title: The compact capsule, its golden, and the verb that renders it
action: Per D6 (as superseded by D19)/D7/D8/D15/D24/D26/D27. STEP 1 — extract three renderers from buildSessionPreamble into exported helpers, output BYTE-IDENTICAL: the onboarding-MISSING line (:318-326), the gate-bypass banner (:342-354), and the HANDOFF block (:369-387). THE HANDOFF RENDERER TAKES BOTH `handoff` AND `handoffOutcome` (D26): handoffOutcome is NOT always null on compact — hooks/bee-session-init.mjs:113-121 sets {ok:false, code:'WRONG_SOURCE'} whenever a planned-next handoff exists on a non-adopting source, and inject.mjs:384-386 renders an `- Adoption not applied: <reason>` line off it. Decide deliberately whether the leading lines.push('') at :375 belongs to the helper or the caller. STEP 2 — add buildCompactCapsule(root, {sessionId, handoffOutcome}) to compaction.mjs. THE handoffOutcome PARAMETER IS MANDATORY AND IS THE POINT OF D27: the hook already computes it at bee-session-init.mjs:113-121 and passes it to buildSessionPreamble at :144; a capsule that omits it silently drops the adoption-refused line for a compacted session holding a planned-next handoff, and NO existing assertion would notice — test_hook_contracts.mjs:2427-2455 and :2864-2872 both regex only the WAIT heading, never the reason line. D6 item 4's 'HANDOFF block verbatim' is only verbatim WITH that line. Emit D6 items 2-12 IN ORDER: STATE MISMATCH when compactCheck fails; onboarding-MISSING; the HANDOFF block (handoff + handoffOutcome); the bypass banner; phase/mode/feature/lane PRESERVING the `- Phase:` label verbatim; the claimed cell with its verify and dependency status; the first open gate; next_action; the recorded commands; the survival count and D9 warning; the critical-patterns pointer. THE CAPSULE NEVER RENDERS THE ANCHOR (D19) — the hook keeps prefixing it. NO CAPSULE BYTE MAY BE ANCHOR-CORRELATED: the additivity row at hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs:2740-2780 compares a with-anchor capsule body against a no-anchor control body, so the STATE MISMATCH line (compactCheck reports 'an anchor exists' as a check — do not let it vary the line), the survival-count line, and blank-line ownership in the HANDOFF arm must all be anchor-independent. STEP 3 — register `state compact-capsule --session-id <id>`: registry entry, handler, dispatch-map line, the bee.mjs:4830 Use-list, and its own assertExampleOk call in test_bee_cli.mjs. STEP 4 — author scripts/test_compact_capsule.mjs: item ORDER asserted positionally; the D15 bare-repo case; a lane-bound session showing its own lane; the STATE MISMATCH path; a row asserting `- Adoption not applied:` appears in the capsule when a planned-next handoff exists (D27); and byte-identity of buildSessionPreamble against a COMMITTED GOLDEN at scripts/fixtures/preamble-golden.txt with the `## bee v${BEE_VERSION}` line (inject.mjs:317) normalized before comparison. Do NOT reconstruct the golden with `git show HEAD:` — that form passes at cap and becomes a tautology for every later cell.  STEP 4b (NAMED MECHANISM, not a delegated aim) — the anchor-independence assertion gets its own row, the way the golden got its own path: render buildCompactCapsule TWICE against paired fixtures identical except that one has an anchor at .bee/intent/ and the other does not, and assert the two outputs are BYTE-EQUAL; include one pair in which compactCheck FAILS, since D12 reports an anchor-exists check and the naive STATE MISMATCH rendering varies with anchor presence by construction. Without this row cz-5 can cap green with an anchor-correlated capsule: hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs:2740-2780 drives the HOOK, which cz-6 has not wired yet, so during cz-5 that row compares full preambles and never touches the capsule. The red would first appear at cz-6, whose files exclude compaction.mjs and which therefore could not legally fix it. NOTE (from cz-4): the four rendered copies of test_bee_cli.mjs under .agents/, .claude/, .claude-plugin/ and .codex-plugin/ are declared in this cell files list on purpose — the D24 render rewrites them from your source edit, and the frozen judge otherwise reads them as undeclared test-source edits. They must stay byte-identical to the source; never hand-edit a projection. STEP 5 — D24 regen chain: mirror, render plugin trees, self-onboard, release_manifest --write.

truths: buildCompactCapsule's signature carries handoffOutcome, and a capsule rendered for a session holding a planned-next handoff contains the `- Adoption not applied:` line — asserted by a test, since no existing row covers it.
buildSessionPreamble's output matches the committed golden for startup, clear and resume, with only the version line normalized.
The golden comparison still bites after cz-5 commits — it is a committed fixture, not a git-show reconstruction.
The capsule's item order is asserted positionally, so reordering fails the suite.

No capsule byte varies with anchor presence, proven by a paired-render byte-equality row inside this cell (including a compactCheck-failing pair) — never deferred to the hook-contract suite, which does not exercise the builder while the hook is un-wired.
The capsule renders on a bare repo emitting no warning and no mismatch line (D15).
The `- Phase:` label is unchanged and hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs is green with ZERO edits.
release_manifest --check and ledger_parity --check are green at cap time.
artifacts: Three renderers, buildCompactCapsule(root,{sessionId,handoffOutcome}), the compact-capsule verb, scripts/test_compact_capsule.mjs, the committed golden, refreshed mirrors and manifest
prohibitions: Never render the anchor inside the capsule (D19).
Never let any capsule byte depend on anchor presence.
Never drop handoffOutcome from the capsule signature or from the HANDOFF renderer.
Never rename or reformat the `- Phase:` label.
Never edit hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs in this cell — if it reds, the capsule is wrong.
Never build the byte-identity baseline from `git show HEAD:`.

verify: node scripts/test_compact_capsule.mjs && node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && node scripts/ledger_parity.mjs --check

trace.outcome: The compact capsule ships: three renderers extracted from buildSessionPreamble (onboardingLine, bypassBannerLines, handoffBlockLines) plus a shared firstOpenGate, proven byte-identical against the committed golden; buildCompactCapsule(root,{sessionId,handoffOutcome}) renders D6 items 2-12 in order with the anchor check muted so no capsule byte varies with anchor presence; the state compact-capsule verb wired end to end (registry entry, handler passing handoffOutcome via nonAdoptingHandoffOutcome, dispatch map, Use list, two assertExampleOk rows); scripts/test_compact_capsule.mjs authored (11 rows incl. the STEP 4b paired-render byte-equality on a failing sweep); D24 regen chain run in order. hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs green with ZERO edits.

Prior behavior characterized: compaction.mjs exported five things and no capsule builder; inject.mjs kept the onboarding line, the bypass banner and the HANDOFF block inlined inside buildSessionPreamble with no exported renderer; `bee state compact-capsule` was an unregistered verb whose registry comment said it was DELIBERATELY NOT registered, and bee.mjs:2400-2402 said the builder ships in a later cell. A SessionStart with source=compact therefore had no compact-scoped orientation body at all -- only the full startup preamble existed. Matches the preserved .bee/tmp/cz-5/red-1.txt from the attempt a concurrent merge destroyed.",
